ops.title: Golden-datum equivalence: cc_steer.build_datum == athome.masked_datum (300/300 real rows)
ops.body:
## Verdict: EQUIVALENT — gates Phase B-1 deletion of cc_steer/retrain/tinker.py

Compared `cc_steer.retrain.tinker.build_datum(messages, mlx_id)` against
`athome.train.data.masked_datum(prompt, completion, mlx_id, weight=1.0)` over
**300 real rows** (first 20 requested, then expanded for confidence — cheap, ~4s
wall) drawn from the live cc-steer watcher training pool
(`~/.cc-steer/dataset/watcher/train.parquet`, 6288 rows total via
`cc_steer.retrain.data.load_train_rows()`), stratified half positive/half negative
(seed 1729). Script: `/tmp/golden_datum_equiv.py` (scratch, never committed).

**Result: 300/300 PASS.** For every row, `tinker.Datum.model_input.to_ints()`,
`loss_fn_inputs["weights"].data`, and `loss_fn_inputs["target_tokens"].data` were
exactly equal (Python `==` on the decoded lists) between the two codepaths. No
divergence found at any token index.

### Matched invocation settings (must hold for the comparison to be valid)
- `mlx_id = "mlx-community/Qwen3-8B-4bit"` — the packaged default
  (`cc_steer/assets/watcher_recipe.json`), also athome's default `MlxModelId`
  (`athome/train/spec.py:77`) and its own test fixture id.
- Both sides resolve the tokenizer via bare `transformers.AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(mlx_id)`
  (`cc_steer/retrain/tinker.py::tokenizer`, `athome/train/data.py::tokenizer`) — same
  underlying call, same cached files.
- Both render via `apply_chat_template(..., tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=<flag>, enable_thinking=False)`
  then `.encode(text, add_special_tokens=False)` — `cc_steer._encode` and
  `athome.chat_ids` are byte-identical logic.
- Messages built exactly as the real call site does:
  `cc_steer/retrain/watcher.py:280` — `tk.build_datum(data.training_sample(row, system=DRAFT_SYSTEM)["messages"], recipe.mlx_id)`.
  `training_sample` (`cc_steer/retrain/data.py:160`) yields `[system, user, assistant]`
  with `user = row.draft_text(cap=DRAFT_CHAR_CAP=10_000)`, `assistant = row.reference`.
- Required env: `HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1` — transformers 5.12.1's `AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained`
  now makes a live Hub call to list `additional_chat_templates` even for a fully
  locally-cached repo; a stale/expired cached HF OAuth token 401s that call. Purely a
  network-check bypass, unrelated to datum construction; both codepaths hit the same
  failure identically before the env var was set.

### The one real semantic difference (accounted for, not a bug)
`athome.masked_datum` takes an explicit `weight: float` keyword (used by
`render_tinker_sft`/`render_tinker_dpo` for oversampling weight); `cc_steer.build_datum`
has no such parameter — its mask is hardcoded to 1.0 on the assistant span. Passing
`weight=1.0` recovers identical behavior; this is the required adaptation when a caller
migrates from `build_datum(messages, mlx_id)` to `masked_datum(messages[:-1], [messages[-1]], mlx_id, weight=1.0)`.

### Boundary-finder equivalence (not just empirically — proven algebraically)
`cc_steer._boundary` (`tinker.py:439`) and `athome.boundary` (`data.py:91`) are the
same longest-common-prefix computation. `_boundary`'s `full[:len(prompt)] == prompt`
early-return branch is a no-op optimization: whenever that condition holds, the
while-loop LCP walk it skips would independently converge to the same `len(prompt)`
value (every index up to `len(prompt)-1` matches by the branch's own premise). The
two are behaviorally identical for every input, confirmed empirically across all 300
sampled rows (identical token-id lengths and identical mask cut points implied by
identical weights arrays).

### Scope note
Only the SFT single-Datum path (`build_datum` vs `masked_datum`) was checked — not
`athome.render_tinker_dpo`/`TinkerPreference`, which cc_steer's `tinker.py` has no
equivalent of (watcher retrain is SFT-only today).
